Wand of Swiftness  
 

Lore Item Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
Charges: 4
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
Effect: Swift Like the Wind (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: Instant) at Level 45
WT: 1.0 Size: SMALL
Class: ENC
Race: ALL except BAR TRL OGR IKS VAH FRG
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Wand of Swiftness
Lore Item Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
Charges: 4
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
Effect: Swift Like the Wind (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: Instant) at Level 45
WT: 1.0 Size: SMALL
Class: ENC
Race: ALL except BAR TRL OGR IKS VAH FRG
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Item Type:Misc
Stackable:No
Merchant Value:10 pp 0 gp 0 sp 0 cp
Tribute:38
Lucy Entry By:hob
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2022-01-13 22:49:07
Page Updated:Wed Apr 26th, 2017

Expansion: Original
Average Price: 450pp Pricing Data...
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 46
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Plane of Hate
NPC Name
a forsaken revenant - male
Coercer T`vala


Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Plane of Hate


Uploaded November 27th, 2008
Send a correction
Post Comment
Usable from inventory by amy class?
QuoteReply
# Sep 15 2003 at 7:10 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592246210091
12 posts
Just wondering if anyone knows for sure that It could be clicked from inventory by any class?
▲ Page top
RE: Usable from inventory by amy class?
QuoteReply
# Nov 15 2003 at 12:55 AM Rating: Excellent
Jaysen
Scholar
46 posts
Just got one tonight and it can't.
▲ Page top
Noggin's Aboggins
QuoteReply
# Apr 05 2003 at 12:32 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592250653639
33 posts
Use your head. Casting Time: Instant
It says nothing about equipping it.I would imagine any class could click this from the inventory slot. Have two characters with one and do the buybacks to recharge it, although I am not sure how much buybacks would run.
▲ Page top
IMHO
QuoteReply
# Feb 17 2003 at 6:48 AM Rating: Good
Nukchuu
107 posts
obviously with 4 charges its not much use, UNTIL you're in one of those sticky situations holding multiple mezzes and your mana is getting low. Oh no!! Melee are screaming for haste!! you have no mana to spare /remezz x 4. Well now isn't this a handy little backup item in your backpack eh? 8P
▲ Page top
RE: IMHO
QuoteReply
# Aug 01 2005 at 12:35 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591842457176
Scholar
36 posts
Hm.. pulling 4 at a time and screaming when they don't get rebuffed right away? Sounds like time to leave that group.. 8)
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Sep 30 2001 at 1:33 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Ok ok a 49 spell that you have to be 56 to even go and get is fine...
RE: newbs
QuoteReply
# Jan 04 2003 at 9:51 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591782049866
143 posts
Can't use the effect till 46 according to a previous post, so no this can not be used to twink. I still think it is a nice item for an enc, as are all the int caster PoH wands. Mine, the wizzy one, is essentially 1866 fire based instant no mana nuke, provided it is chain clicked, and the necro one is nice also, same example, only apx 1000 lifetap. Haven't read about the mage one yet.
▲ Page top
Not exactly mighty..
QuoteReply
# Jun 11 2001 at 6:58 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
...So we use them to buff the clerics ;)
▲ Page top
mana battery
QuoteReply
# May 29 2001 at 8:53 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
any one try to combin this with the grom manna battery. now a days the idea will be less dangeros, as if it will not work u will not lose the item. just a thought, thanks four your time, safe hunting)
▲ Page top
decent item
QuoteReply
# May 27 2001 at 4:58 PM Rating: Decent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
About equal in usefulness to the Wand of Allure. This allows a 46 enchanter to cast a 49 spell. Handy, not overpowering, and useful for group situations. But with 4 charges, its not like anyone is going to be selling hordes of these at 460pp like the site states.
▲ Page top
RE: decent item
QuoteReply
# May 27 2001 at 8:30 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592756927550
26 posts
Mana Required: 250

That's 1000 mana this wand saves you. If it's instant cast like people are saying, so much the better. A nifty item to get... probably not worth saving the charges, especially if you are level 49. Just use them when you need to haste the tanks in your group, and sell the wand to a vendor when you've used all four charges ;)
▲ Page top
Swift like the Wind
QuoteReply
# May 26 2001 at 7:01 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Increase Attack Speed by 60% (L49)


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Classes: Enc (L49)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Range to Target: 100 feet

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Skill: Alteration
Allowable Targets: All

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Resistance Check: None

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Mana Required: 250
Spell Duration: 160 ticks (16.0 minutes)
Duration Formula: 10
Casting Time: 8.0 seconds
Spell Recovery: 2.25 seconds

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Spell cast on you: You feel much faster.
Spell cast on someone: Soandso feels much faster.
Spell fades: Your speed returns to normal.
▲ Page top
4 charges
QuoteReply
# May 26 2001 at 2:13 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Bah!! 4 charges sucks it big time. This item would rock the house if it didnt have 4 chages.
▲ Page top
RE: 4 charges
QuoteReply
# Jul 02 2002 at 8:08 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592759278476
23 posts
As opposed to no charges huh.
▲ Page top
?
QuoteReply
# May 25 2001 at 5:43 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
"Two spells are special in that....so piddly they're not worth it"

You nuts? EVERY % of haste you can add on counts..any monk with the Epic or ROTWF knows what I'm talking about...stack item haste+spell haste+bard song haste....we learn it early
▲ Page top
FYI
QuoteReply
# May 25 2001 at 3:22 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
There is no difference between spell haste and song haste.

Two songs are special in that they stack with spells but they're so piddly and pathetic it's not near halfway worth it...
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: May 25 2001 at 5:00 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) umm, excuse me? you must either be a moron or have never even met a post 5th level bard!! at 7th level the first bard haste song already makes us faster than an fbss. try playing the class before you assume. fool.
RE: FYI
QuoteReply
# May 26 2001 at 3:24 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592245623402
6 posts
Rhysling, before insulting someone, you should read what he wrote.. He never said that bards don't have haste songs worth using. He said that spell haste and song haste don't stack (wich, if u prefer, means your haste songs are useless when goruped wiht an ench). There are indeed 2 songs that will stack, but losing one of the 3 songs a bard can play to add such a low % of haste is quite bad.
So I will give you 2 advices :
- consult this link, wirtten by AL's guildleader and dealing about bard's haste stacking problems :
http://www.afterlifeguild.org/Thott/haste_stacking.html
- learn politeness.

Ektarot, mage 56, MM.
▲ Page top
this wand
QuoteReply
# May 25 2001 at 2:52 PM Rating: Good
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Knuckleheads , try using your noggins for something other then coming up with funny insults

Think a sec...your in hate...this drops...*lightbulb* Hay i can haste all my front line tanks for no mana!! OR dammit that mob just dispelled all the tanks,and i cant waste mana during the fight to rehaste...WALIT a sec i got this cool rod...

Think a lil guys,you can usually come up with neat uses for just about anything
▲ Page top

A wand of swiftness was the first thing I ever got from a plane. I named it Bernice. I will treasure her always.

QuoteReply
# May 24 2001 at 7:25 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Considering that an enchanter can't even get this until 46 (yeah I guess you could hand it to a twink I guess...) this is highly lame. It would be rather neat if say a melee class could use it though...
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: May 24 2001 at 5:30 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) this is such a tight item. 10 pp to a vendor you say? nice.
What % haste?
QuoteReply
# May 24 2001 at 4:25 PM Rating: Decent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
What percent haste is this? Does the effect count as a spell haste, a song haste, or an item haste?
▲ Page top
RE: What % haste?
QuoteReply
# May 24 2001 at 4:51 PM Rating: Excellent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Swift like the wind is 60% spell haste. Enchanters get this spell at 49th level, but the wand is manaless, no cast time, and sells for 10 pp to a vendor.
